scenes.title: Kitsoki Roadmap
scenes.subtitle: Stable base, reinforcement gaps, exhaustive upcoming work, and explicit gates
scenes.narration: Kitsoki roadmap.

scenes.title: Roadmap lens

cards.label: Stable base
lines: agent verbs, launch, CodeAct, and policy
story, worklist, graph, and no-LLM proof

cards.label: New capabilities
cards.sub: new work visible in git, goals, docs, stories, tests, decks, and feature YAML

cards.label: In progress
cards.sub: partially real slices with named reinforcement gaps

cards.label: Coming soon
cards.sub: next bets with source-backed dependency order

cards.label: Waiting on
cards.sub: explicit spend, quota, hosting, sandbox, and media gates

scenes.caption: Every claim should point back to a catalog entry, proposal status line, trace, deck, or deterministic check.
scenes.narration: Roadmap as evidence: stable base, new capabilities, in progress, coming soon, and explicit gates.

scenes.title: Roadmap lanes
scenes.caption: Roadmap items graduate when runtime, proof surface, real workflow, and learning loop all have owners.
scenes.narration: The roadmap has four product lanes: control plane, proof surfaces, real work, and the learning loop. The feature catalog and proposal queue keep those lanes honest.

scenes.title: Stable base: control path

items.label: Workflow runtime and operation handles
items.detail: state, guards, nudges, resubmission, bounded calls, background jobs, and drive controls are runtime-owned instead of ad hoc agent behavior

items.label: Agent verbs and operator interaction
items.detail: task, decide, ask, converse, tool streams, direct submits, and operator-ask give the system explicit mechanisms instead of silent empty answers

items.label: Deterministic tracing and playback
items.detail: tree, timeline, graph, decision detail, confidence, alternatives, annotation, and replay are visible from the recorded trace

scenes.caption: Workflow runtime, operation handles, agent interaction mechanisms, and deterministic trace playback are visible product surfaces today.
scenes.narration: The public proof path starts with workflow runtime, operation handles, agent interaction mechanisms, and deterministic trace playback.

scenes.title: Stable base: agent and workflow launch

items.label: Kitsoki agent launch
items.detail: story and freestanding agents resolve to dry-runable Claude, Codex, Copilot, or CodeAct command plans with backend, model, tools, env, cwd, and prompt visible before execution

items.label: Launch policy
items.detail: protected checkouts, protected branches, and non-capsule workspaces are rejected before an external agent starts; decisions are traceable when a sink is attached

items.label: Studio MCP and dynamic workflows
items.detail: external agents can author stories, drive sessions, inspect traces, and turn proposal-shaped goals into validated, launched, exportable workflow receipts

scenes.caption: Agent launch, launch policy, Studio MCP, and dynamic workflows are available control-plane features, not future bets.
scenes.narration: Agent launch, launch policy, Studio MCP, and dynamic workflows are stable control-plane surfaces.

scenes.title: Stable base: proof in use

items.label: Self-improvement
items.detail: completed runs can file trace-backed improvement reports through the same meta surface

items.label: Real repo work
items.detail: dev-story and Slidey case studies show triage, bugfix, PRD, design, decomposition, refinement, PR, and merge-shaped workflows

items.label: Local evaluation
items.detail: stranger install, truthful first-run guidance, and kitsoki doctor make the first local run inspectable instead of a silent stub

scenes.caption: Self-improvement, real workflow demos, and local trial close the public proof path.
scenes.narration: Self improvement, real repo workflows, and local evaluation close the public proof path.

scenes.title: Stable base: under-advertised systems

items.label: Scenario proof packaging
items.detail: scenario packs, semantic refs, completion state, readiness reports, and product-learning reports are in-tree; the remaining work is product projection and publication

items.label: CodeAct action surface
items.detail: host.agent.codeact, mcp-codeact, CodeAct launch mode, direct-API agent tests, replay, and arena treatment wiring exist; catalog/docs/demo promotion still needs cleanup

items.label: Local artifact bug loop
items.detail: kitsoki bug create/list/show can use the local-artifact sink so dogfood loops stay local until GitHub is the handoff boundary

items.label: Generic semantic annotation
items.detail: ArtifactAnnotator and generic HTML semantic replay tests prove the anchor model is broader than Slidey-only deck refinement

scenes.caption: These systems are already in the codebase, but the product catalog and roadmap have not caught up.
scenes.narration: Several important systems are already real but under-advertised: scenario proof packaging, CodeAct, local artifact bug filing, and generic semantic annotation.

scenes.title: Feature catalog: runtime and launch

cells: agent-actions, operator-ask, operation handles, trace features
The model is a bounded callee and long work has handles.
agent launch, launch policy, harness picker, CodeAct mode
Backend launches are inspectable, policy-checked, and profile-stamped.
Setup truth is a product surface.

scenes.caption: The first catalog group covers control, agent launch, and first-run truth.
scenes.narration: The feature catalog starts with controlled runtime behavior, safe agent launch, and truthful first-run setup.

scenes.title: Feature catalog: authoring and work

cells: Studio MCP, meta mode, story editor, dynamic workflows, punch-list, goal-seeker
Stories, worklists, and workflow drafts are editable, launchable, and checkable.
chat stream, operation handles, inbox, media workbench, review

scenes.caption: The next catalog group covers authoring, long-running operation, and real workflow proof.
scenes.narration: The catalog then maps authoring, long-running operation, and case-study proof.

scenes.title: Feature catalog: gap register

cells: CodeAct, scenario proof packaging, scenario QA, product-journey QA, arena/swarm, dogfood marathon, dev-story mining, local artifact bug loop, generic semantic annotation
Add catalog pages or mark internal-only with rationale.
Existing feature YAML without demo binding
Bind generalized-usage clips or add explicit no-demo status.

scenes.caption: A complete roadmap needs the gap register beside the catalog, not hidden in review notes.
scenes.narration: The catalog is valid, but product coverage still has page and demo-binding gaps.

scenes.title: Feature catalog: proof gaps

cells: Most recordable features have capture specs but no checked-out generated artifacts; meta-improvement is the durable local example
Run targeted captures for promo-grid claims and rely on site CI presence gates.
complete-product-tour is metadata only; deleted external-stub features were intentionally delisted
Do not re-add unrecordable demos until replay/cassette strategy changes.

scenes.caption: Proof gaps are catalog work: missing replay artifacts and known exceptions need explicit ownership.
scenes.narration: Proof gaps are catalog work too: missing replay artifacts and known demo exceptions need explicit ownership.

scenes.title: New capabilities: proof systems

items.label: Meta improvement promoted
items.detail: improvement reports moved into the product site, feature catalog, docs, server, and web surfaces

items.label: Persona QA productized
items.detail: scenario QA now has natural requests, transport suites, evidence levels, report closeout, and rrweb proof

items.label: Arena treatments extracted
items.detail: raw Codex, direct CodeAct, Studio MCP, and Studio MCP plus CodeAct are distinct action surfaces with no-LLM validation

scenes.caption: Recent work strengthened the proof layer: meta reports, Persona QA, and arena surfaces.
scenes.narration: Recent work strengthened the proof layer with meta improvement, Persona QA, and arena treatments.

scenes.title: New capabilities: authoring discipline

items.label: Slidey editing hardened
items.detail: deck editing agents are restricted to Slidey MCP and workspace isolation was fixed

items.label: Dev-story mining started
items.detail: a dev-story transcript mining story now exists with rooms, schemas, prompts, and flows

items.label: Media and workspace discipline tightened
items.detail: rrweb-first media defaults, staging capsule flow, TUI selector, and Studio MCP friction fixes landed together

scenes.caption: Recent infrastructure work made deck editing, transcript mining, media, and workspaces more durable.
scenes.narration: Recent infrastructure work hardened Slidey editing, dev-story mining, media, and workspace discipline.

scenes.title: Generalized usage: shipped and live

items.label: Goal-seeker loop
items.detail: Kitsoki can evaluate a decomposed goal, dispatch workers, integrate green work, and re-evaluate instead of trusting self-report.

items.label: Kitsoki agent launch
items.detail: Operators can inspect and run story-backed or freestanding Claude, Codex, Copilot, and CodeAct launches through one command surface.

items.label: Stranger install
items.detail: A released binary can run inside a foreign repo, profile it, install toolkit wiring, and avoid a source checkout dependency.

items.label: Truthful local start
items.detail: Missing providers, unavailable actions, and doctor preflights render explicit setup guidance instead of pretending the workflow is live.

items.label: Story editor
items.detail: Rooms, hooks, domain model, and Agent Workbench are inspectable without going on a YAML expedition.

scenes.caption: These are already product surfaces with deterministic checks behind them.
scenes.narration: Generalized usage is not a future theme. Goal seeker, stranger install, truthful first run, doctor, and the story editor are live surfaces.

scenes.title: Active goals: roadmap map

cells: goal-seeker, stranger install, truthful first run, doctor, agent launch, story editor
Bind clips to feature demos, prove foreign-repo targeting, close cost-proof and retrospective surfaces.
host.agent.codeact, mcp-codeact, bounded Starlark loop, replay, promotion ratchet, arena treatments
Promote to feature page, docs reference, demo proof, and launch/sandbox comparison.

scenes.caption: The goal files are roadmap inputs. Each needs an advertised product surface plus reinforcement gates.
scenes.narration: Generalized usage and CodeAct already have code behind them; the roadmap names the reinforcement each still needs.

scenes.title: Active goals: proof systems

cells: persona QA, scenario QA, arena runner, swarm package, completion-state adapters
Unify completion state, run persona journeys through arena, prove 24+ simulated users without live spend by default.
scenario packs, scenario QA, product-journey QA, readiness and product-learning report outputs
Unify feature catalog, scenario runs, capture events, and generated site-page publishing.

scenes.caption: The proof goals need unified completion state, capture contracts, and publishing gates.
scenes.narration: UI QA scale and the scenario product platform already have substrate; the roadmap names the product proof still needed.

scenes.title: Trace observability: decisions

items.label: Agent action transcripts
items.detail: per-call sidecars preserve reasoning and tool use; rollups make the full run scanable

items.label: Decision-first trace detail
items.detail: verdict, confidence, alternatives, and evidence drawers put the decision edge before raw prompt text

items.label: Live trace features
items.detail: view modes, waterfall, observation category chips, annotation, and replay are part of the live observer story

scenes.caption: The trace layer exposes action transcripts, decision detail, and live replay surfaces.
scenes.narration: Trace observability starts with action transcripts, decision-first detail, and live trace features.

scenes.title: Trace observability: operations

items.label: Operation handles and inbox
items.detail: long-running work gets visible handles, notifications, and jump-back affordances

items.label: Session media workbench
items.detail: artifacts stay pinned while chat and devtools keep moving, which makes review workflows possible

scenes.caption: Long-running operations stay reviewable through handles, inbox, and media workbench surfaces.
scenes.narration: Long-running operations stay reviewable through operation handles, inbox, and the media workbench.

scenes.title: Authoring loop: edit and improve

items.label: Meta mode
items.detail: a running state machine can pause into a persistent side conversation that edits and hot-reloads the story

items.label: Improvement reports
items.detail: surprising runs become evidence-backed reports with prompt, tool, script, and permission lessons

items.label: Story editor
items.detail: the web UI exposes the room map, hook/domain-model detail, and Agent Workbench cassette browser

scenes.caption: Meta mode, improvement reports, and the story editor make stories inspectable and repairable.
scenes.narration: Authoring starts with meta mode, improvement reports, and the story editor.

scenes.title: Authoring loop: orchestrate work

items.label: Dynamic workflows
items.detail: proposal-shaped goals can become validated workflow drafts and live sessions; structured decomposition, first-class parallel groups, worker receipts, and final reports still need hardening

items.label: Punch-list and goal-seeker
items.detail: YAML worklists and bounded goal loops turn backlog work into replayable story execution

scenes.caption: Dynamic workflows, punch-list, and goal-seeker turn backlog work into replayable execution; dynamic workflow fan-out and receipt folding are the reinforcement work.
scenes.narration: Backlog work is replayable; dynamic workflow fan-out and receipt folding still need hardening.

scenes.title: Evaluation proof: QA and demos

items.label: Persona QA story
items.detail: preview, check, transport-pinned legs, independent judge outcomes, report.md, and deck.slidey.json are the public flow

items.label: Product-site demos
items.detail: feature YAML drives live tour overlays, rrweb captures, step screenshots, site pages, and QA scenarios

items.label: Arena comparisons
items.detail: treatments are explicit action surfaces; spec validation is no-LLM and live spend is gated

scenes.caption: Persona QA, product-site demos, and arena comparisons form the front of the proof chain.
scenes.narration: Persona QA, product-site demos, and arena comparisons form the front of the proof chain.

scenes.title: Evaluation proof: artifacts

items.label: Slidey decks
items.detail: committed decks under docs/decks can publish through the product-site gallery and embed rrweb clips

items.label: UI demo and UI QA loop
items.detail: deterministic replay and skeptical frame review turn demos into product gates, not marketing afterthoughts

scenes.caption: Slidey decks and UI demo/QA gates turn proof into reviewable artifacts.
scenes.narration: Slidey decks and UI demo or QA gates turn proof into reviewable artifacts.

scenes.title: Evaluation proof: scenario platform

items.label: Scenario Product Platform
items.detail: the story emits scenario-pack/v1 bundles, semantic refs, report.md, deck.slidey.json, completion-state.json, Release Readiness, Product Learning, and generated site-page output

items.label: Scenario QA
items.detail: one scenario can be checked across TUI, web, and VS Code transports with per-leg driver and judge evidence

items.label: Product journey QA
items.detail: persona and scenario matrices drive product proof bundles, dogfood runs, and no-LLM replay gates before live capture

items.label: Arena and swarm bridge
items.detail: arena treatments and swarm users are present; the next product slice is one shared completion-state and capture contract

scenes.caption: The product-proof stack is converging around scenarios, not one-off demos.
scenes.narration: The next proof platform should unite scenario packs, scenario QA, product journey QA, arena, swarm, rrweb, and Slidey reports.

scenes.title: Case studies: engineering workflows

items.label: Dev-story bugfix
items.detail: triage a ticket, hand it to the bugfix pipeline, then watch reproduce, propose, implement, test, review, validate, and PR-ready states

items.label: Slidey developer arc
items.detail: PM idea, architect design, decomposition, bugfix, spatial refine, and PR scenes are committed as a deck-local rrweb story arc

items.label: Bugfix bakeoff
items.detail: methodology is validated, first cells are scored, and blocked/pending cells remain explicit instead of hidden

scenes.caption: Bugfix, Slidey development, and bakeoff examples exercise real engineering workflow shapes.
scenes.narration: Engineering case studies cover bugfix, Slidey development, and bakeoff workflow shapes.

scenes.title: Case studies: other domains

items.label: Weather Starlark host
items.detail: deterministic HTTP glue runs through host.starlark.run with cassettes and trace-visible calls

items.label: Mockup and design workflows
items.detail: PRD, design, mockup video, and anchored Slidey refine flows prove non-bugfix domains share the same engine

scenes.caption: Starlark HTTP glue, PRD, design, and mockup workflows show the engine is not bugfix-only.
scenes.narration: Other domain case studies include Starlark HTTP glue, PRD, design, and mockup workflows.

scenes.title: In progress: integrations

items.label: Onboarding surfaces
items.detail: TUI onboarding is proven; web, VS Code, GitHub-originated starts, and deeper repo analysis still need the same setup flow

items.label: GitHub agent and reports
items.detail: issue ingress and bugfix dispatch are real for issues, while PR autopilot and web operator viewer remain in the proposal queue

items.label: Artifact-driven stories
items.detail: local artifact-job registry and rebuildable run/artifact index exist; unified instances, publish lifecycle, adoption, and console remain open

scenes.caption: Onboarding, GitHub agent work, and artifact-driven stories are partially real and still being integrated.
scenes.narration: In progress begins with onboarding, GitHub agent reports, and artifact-driven stories.

scenes.title: In progress: hardening

items.label: Contextual routing polish
items.detail: runtime slices shipped; switch-route ergonomics, TUI receipt/rewind parity, and extra fixtures remain

items.label: Typed view readability
items.detail: typed views are widely wired, but legacy templates, direct TUI rendering, trace/web typed recording, and a proofing command remain

items.label: Arena live path
items.detail: no-LLM treatment validation is strong; VM pools, plugin breadth, completion polling, and old command retirement remain

scenes.caption: Routing, typed view readability, and arena live paths are hardening work with named proposal gaps.
scenes.narration: The hardening lane covers contextual routing, typed view readability, and arena live execution.

scenes.title: Proposal queue: memory and collaboration

cells: catalog loader, changesets, feature/proposal adapters, diff tooling
initiative views, area inbox, category nav, roadmap deltas
feature page, docs reference, demo, sandbox comparison
PR autopilot, operator viewer, durable run

scenes.caption: The first queue cluster turns project memory and collaboration into durable product surfaces.
scenes.narration: The first proposal cluster covers project catalog memory, human actions, and the GitHub agent.

scenes.title: Proposal queue: artifacts and feedback

cells: neutral SDK, privacy manifest, generic sink
scenario pack, semantic refs, readiness reports
catalog integration, arena rollups, site pages

scenes.caption: The middle queue cluster makes artifacts and feedback portable across workflows.
scenes.narration: The middle proposal cluster covers artifact jobs, the feedback SDK, and session mining.

scenes.title: Proposal queue: training and channels

cells: oracle mining, training loop, story integration
live matrices, strict cassettes, runtime pin selection

scenes.caption: The final queue cluster turns stories into trainable assets and expands beyond developer tooling.
scenes.narration: The final proposal cluster covers trainable stories, LINE, repo-history learning, and agent contract evaluation.

scenes.title: Proposal queue: hardening and docs

cells: artifact mode, observation kinds, decision detail
single-file export, virtualization, live SSE, consumer rewrite
legacy fallback trim, direct TUI rendering, proof command
kit commands, kit verification, docs index

scenes.caption: The roadmap includes hardening work that rarely fits a feature card but blocks trust.
scenes.narration: The first hardening cluster covers runstatus, trace fidelity, typed views, kits, and library docs.

scenes.title: Proposal queue: runtime hardening

cells: engine/CLI slices and several continue waves
pre-bind staging, bugfix migration, recovery-state cleanup
provider abstraction and CodeAct direct-API seams
spike, model selection, no-LLM fixtures, operator docs

scenes.caption: Runtime hardening remains roadmap work even when it is not a flashy feature surface.
scenes.narration: The second hardening cluster covers execution modes, continue mode, and local model work.

scenes.title: Coming soon: safety and memory

items.label: Capability sandbox and harness identity
items.detail: carry launch policy from preflight into enforceable runtime identity, protected roots, protected branches, opened-capsule checks, and trace receipts

items.label: Task filesystem sandbox
items.detail: pick the confinement backend below host tools so write agents and external agents cannot bypass the effect/toolbox model

items.label: Portable feedback SDK
items.detail: turn the current browser bug-report and Slidey report flow into a privacy-first framework-neutral feedback SDK

items.label: Project catalog and roadmap deltas
items.detail: make features, proposals, plans, tests, evidence, and roadmap changes typed project records instead of scattered prose

scenes.caption: The next safety bets start with launch policy becoming real confinement, plus portable feedback and typed memory.
scenes.narration: Coming soon starts with capability sandboxing, harness identity, filesystem confinement, portable feedback, and project catalog deltas.

scenes.title: Coming soon: expansion paths

items.label: Scenario product platform
items.detail: unify feature catalog, product journeys, scenario QA, arena, semantic refs, rrweb, Slidey reports, and readiness/product-learning reports

items.label: API and library docs
items.detail: turn docs index, host registry, opschema, kits, story metadata, and extension docs into an inspectable /library product surface

items.label: Human action workflows
items.detail: put human-owned tasks beside agent-owned work with host.human verbs, GitHub Issue backend, and mixed decomposition

items.label: Trainable stories
items.detail: convert traces, failures, credit assignment, and validation gates into a repeatable optimizer step for story changes

items.label: Customer channels
items.detail: LINE ingress, transport, commerce stories, and operator handoff would prove Kitsoki outside developer tooling

scenes.caption: Scenario productization, API docs, human actions, trainable stories, and customer channels extend the same engine outward.
scenes.narration: The expansion path covers scenario productization, API and library docs, human action workflows, trainable stories, and customer channels.

scenes.title: Coming soon: reinforcement backlog

cells: missing YAML pages, missing demo bindings, and mostly generated rrweb artifacts absent from the checkout
feature YAML or internal-only decision, then capture or explicit no-demo status
CodeAct exists in code but is uneven across host refs, story-authoring refs, sandbox comparison, and catalog docs
docs update plus feature page and no-LLM launch/replay gate

scenes.caption: Reinforcement work is part of the roadmap: tests, docs, demos, and source-of-truth decisions are not cleanup trivia.
scenes.narration: The first reinforcement cluster covers feature catalog, demo bindings, and CodeAct documentation.

scenes.title: Coming soon: test and demo gates

cells: live eval runner, web-shot start-state support, TUI rendering simulation, and Studio replay/frame tools are deferred
focused no-LLM tests before any user-facing claim graduates
TUI and free-text demos need universal-scenario ownership and replay recordings rather than bespoke proof
scenario bundle, rrweb or xterm rrweb capture, media-check, and deck/source bundle

scenes.caption: Runtime tests and demo proof are graduation gates, not follow-up chores.
scenes.narration: The second reinforcement cluster covers runtime tests and demo proof gates.

scenes.title: Waiting on: spend and recordings

items.label: Live LLM and vision spend
items.detail: automated tests stay no-LLM; live model and vision review only run when an operator explicitly chooses that gate

items.label: Provider quota and cost walls
items.detail: bakeoff and arena cells may be quota-blocked or pending; blocked cells remain first-class evidence, not zeroes

items.label: Free-text demo recordings
items.detail: conversational product demos need replay recordings or cassettes for slot extraction; Slidey hybrid phases 1-3 should either get recordings or be narrowed to the committed developer arc

scenes.caption: Live model spend, quota walls, and replayable free-text recordings are explicit gates.
scenes.narration: Waiting starts with live spend, provider quota, and replayable free-text recordings.

scenes.title: Waiting on: infrastructure gates

items.label: Hosted artifact decisions
items.detail: durable artifact jobs need storage, auth, locking, publish/archive, and run identity decisions before hosted workflows are complete

items.label: Secure confinement backend
items.detail: write/external agents need a selected sandbox runtime ladder, harness identity, and reference-monitor story before capability enforcement is complete

items.label: Responsive media variants
items.detail: mobile and tablet demo captures wait until each feature is genuinely responsive, not a scaled desktop replay

scenes.caption: Hosted artifact policy, secure confinement, and responsive media variants are infrastructure decisions.
scenes.narration: The remaining gates are hosted artifact policy, secure confinement, and responsive media variants.

scenes.title: Roadmap dependency graph
scenes.caption: Scenario-backed proof feeds gates, gates feed project memory, and proven memory/work unlock docs, collaboration, and channels.
scenes.narration: Proof feeds gates; gates feed project memory; memory and work unlock docs, collaboration, and channels.

scenes.title: Golden deck quality: evidence

items.label: Source-backed
items.detail: The deck is grounded in git history, product-site pages, feature YAML, proposal statuses, docs, and .context notes.

items.label: Status vocabulary reused
items.detail: It uses the existing evidence/objectives status format: done, validated, progress, next, blocked, implemented.

items.label: Roadmap oriented
items.detail: It separates stable base, new capabilities, in progress, coming soon, and waiting on explicit gates.

items.label: Engineer-readable groups
items.detail: It groups runtime, proof, work, learning, catalog debt, proposals, blockers, and reinforcement work instead of dumping an unprioritized backlog.

scenes.caption: The deck should be source-backed, use familiar statuses, stay roadmap oriented, and read like an engineering plan.
scenes.narration: A golden deck should be source backed, reuse the project status vocabulary, stay roadmap oriented, and read like an engineering plan.

scenes.title: Golden deck quality: publishing

items.label: No fake proof
items.detail: Blocked or pending work is named as blocked or pending rather than presented as shipped.

items.label: Slidey validation path
items.detail: The deck source validates through Slidey and the product-site media contract.

items.label: Publish-ready source
items.detail: The source deck and bundled gallery viewer are committed together under docs/decks/.

items.label: Reinforcement visible
items.detail: Missing tests, demos, docs, feature YAML, and proof ownership are named as roadmap work rather than hidden as cleanup.

scenes.caption: The deck should name blocked work honestly, validate through Slidey, publish from committed source, and expose reinforcement work.
scenes.narration: A golden deck should avoid fake proof, validate through Slidey, publish from source, and expose reinforcement work.
Kitsoki roadmap as evidence, not vibes.
